assertion.summary: Feature-owned proof mapping for the kill-worker lifecycle. It ties the checked-in Mission-GUI-TEST Electron session evidence for the real Mission session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` to focused automated coverage for `droid.kill_worker_session`, `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user")`, and the resulting paused/resumable Mission UX.

automated.case: Mission Control timeline distinguishes user kill and daemon failure events
automated.verifies: Mission progress renders `Worker killed by user` followed by `Mission paused`, proving the runtime timeline distinguishes `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` from daemon failure.

automated.case: kill messaging distinguishes user-killed workers from daemon failures and acknowledges pending kill
automated.verifies: The UI immediately acknowledges Kill Worker with pending feedback, then exposes paused-after-user-kill recovery copy saying the worker was terminated at the user's request, leaving the Mission inspectable and resumable.

automated.case: mission session plumbing preserves agi/orchestrator settings and supports kill_worker_session
automated.verifies: The real JSON-RPC request path sends `droid.kill_worker_session` for the active Mission session and preserves Mission metadata before and after the worker-kill operation.

interactive.evidence: Created the real Mission-GUI-TEST Mission session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` through the Electron surface after explicitly selecting Custom -> `CCH-GPT-5.4`; the persisted session file `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391.json` recorded `isMission=true`, `sessionKind=mission`, `interactionMode=agi`, and `decompSessionType=orchestrator`.

Re-opened that same Mission session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` in Mission-GUI-TEST and toggled between Chat and Mission Control without changing sessions, proving the Mission remained inspectable on the Electron surface before and after runtime-control checks.

The checked-in `runtime-controls-kill-pending.png` capture shows immediate Kill Worker acknowledgement with the control disabled before async completion. The same flow also records a paused Chat screenshot for session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` with the normal input enabled, proving the Mission remains resumable on the same session after the user-kill path.

supportingContext: The Mission-GUI-TEST runtime-controls flow explicitly notes that destructive kill execution was exercised via isolated renderer-state injection to avoid mutating the shared validator-phase run. This feature-owned proof does not present that injected state as sole proof; instead it combines the new checked-in owner mapping here with real same-session Electron Mission-GUI-TEST evidence for the underlying session identity/inspectability and with focused automated kill-worker coverage.
The broader `.factory/validation/mission-verification/feature-owned-proof-followup.json` bundle remains supporting context only and is no longer the owner for VAL-CROSS-005.

proofOwnershipNotes: This file is the new checked-in feature-owned deliverable for `mission-cross-flow-kill-worker-pause-proof`.
It intentionally narrows ownership to VAL-CROSS-005 instead of relying on the superseded combined follow-up proof bundle.
The Mission-GUI-TEST evidence cited here stays on the Electron surface with the required isolated app-data directory and Custom `CCH-GPT-5.4` model selection.
